info.title: Canon Keeper Backend-first API
info.summary: Client/server contract for Canon Keeper projects, books, chapters and collaboration.
info.description: Backend-first OpenAPI 3.1 contract for Canon Keeper. Canonical surfaces are resources, commands, private queries, shared jobs and resumable chat streams; current client compatibility is documented in migration notes, not treated as API authority.

tags.name: Projects
tags.description: Project tenant boundary, membership and invitations.

tags.name: Manuscript
tags.description: Books, chapters, annotations, revisions and locators.

tags.name: Jobs
tags.description: Shared async lifecycle for import, indexing, export, chat and agent work.

tags.name: Chat
tags.description: Chats, turn commands, snapshots and resumable SSE.

post.description: Creates a new account/session with strict local Origin and CSRF before cookie mutation. Responses are no-store and do not expose existing account state.
post.x-authz: Anonymous registration command creates a new account/session without exposing existing account state.

post.description: Exchanges credentials with strict local Origin and CSRF before cookie mutation. Errors are non-enumerating and responses are no-store.
post.x-authz: Anonymous credential exchange returns the same non-enumerating errors for unknown users or wrong credentials.

post.description: Invalidates the current session with strict local Origin and CSRF before clearing the cookie. Responses are no-store.
post.x-authz: Authenticated session owner only; anonymous callers receive 401 and no project authorization is evaluated.

logoutNoContent.summary: Synthetic logout clears session and rotates CSRF

get.summary: List projects available to the user.
get.x-authz: Authenticated user lists only projects where they already have membership; non-member projects are omitted.

post.x-authz: Authenticated user creates a new project and becomes initial owner; no pre-existing project membership or direct-ID lookup is required.

get.x-authz: Project member read on the owning resource: viewer/editor/admin/owner allowed; non-member direct ids return leak-safe 404.

patch.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner mutation on the owning project resource; viewer receives 403 and non-member direct ids return 404.

delete.x-authz: Owner/admin only tenant deletion; editor/viewer receive 403 and non-member/cross-project project ids return leak-safe 404.

get.summary: List books in a project shelf.
get.x-authz: Project member read on the owning resource: viewer/editor/admin/owner allowed; non-member direct ids return leak-safe 404.

post.summary: Create a book inside a project.
post.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner mutation on the owning project resource; viewer receives 403 and non-member direct ids return 404.

get.summary: Get a book with current indexing status.
get.x-authz: Project member read on the owning resource: viewer/editor/admin/owner allowed; non-member direct ids return leak-safe 404.

patch.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner mutation on the owning project resource; viewer receives 403 and non-member direct ids return 404.

delete.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner mutation on the owning project resource; viewer receives 403 and non-member direct ids return 404.

200.description: Upload constraints accepted by the backend.

get.x-authz: Project member read on the owning resource: viewer/editor/admin/owner allowed; non-member direct ids return leak-safe 404.

post.description: Direct multipart upload with file plus metadata/options. URL-fetch and uploaded-file-id imports are out of scope; upload controls require server-generated storage key, filename non-use as path, content sniffing, archive bounds and parser quarantine.
post.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner may import manuscript files into the project; viewer receives 403 and non-member/cross-project project ids return leak-safe 404.

post.description: Owner/admin full-manuscript export start. Editors/viewers receive 403; output is no-store/private and retention is documented in security matrix.
post.x-authz: Owner/admin only for full manuscript export start; editor/viewer receive 403 and cross-project book ids return leak-safe 404.

get.summary: List chapter structure for a book.
get.x-authz: Project member read on the owning resource: viewer/editor/admin/owner allowed; non-member direct ids return leak-safe 404.

post.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner mutation on the owning project resource; viewer receives 403 and non-member direct ids return 404.

get.summary: Get chapter content for published or draft editing.

parameters.name: contentVariant
parameters.description: Select published or draft chapter content without changing the chapter lifecycle state.

chapter.summary: Synthetic chapter resource with bounded paragraph text

get.x-authz: Project member read on the owning resource: viewer/editor/admin/owner allowed; non-member direct ids return leak-safe 404.

patch.summary: Save chapter title, draft body, or ordering metadata.
patch.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner mutation on the owning project resource; viewer receives 403 and non-member direct ids return 404.

delete.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner mutation on the owning project resource; viewer receives 403 and non-member direct ids return 404.

post.summary: Publish the current draft revision into the reader version.
post.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner mutation on the owning project resource; viewer receives 403 and non-member direct ids return 404.

get.summary: List reader annotations anchored to a chapter.
get.x-authz: Project member read on the owning resource: viewer/editor/admin/owner allowed; non-member direct ids return leak-safe 404.

post.summary: Create a reader annotation anchored to a locator.
post.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner mutation on the owning project resource; viewer receives 403 and non-member direct ids return 404.

patch.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner mutation on the owning project resource; viewer receives 403 and non-member direct ids return 404.

delete.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner mutation on the owning project resource; viewer receives 403 and non-member direct ids return 404.

post.description: Read-only POST body query. Query text stays out of URLs; cursor is bound to request fingerprint; snippets are bounded and synthetic in examples.
post.x-authz: Project member read on the owning resource: viewer/editor/admin/owner allowed; non-member direct ids return leak-safe 404.

get.summary: List chat sessions for a project.
get.x-authz: Project member read on the owning resource: viewer/editor/admin/owner allowed; non-member direct ids return leak-safe 404.

post.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner mutation on the owning project resource; viewer receives 403 and non-member direct ids return 404.

get.x-authz: Project member read on the owning resource: viewer/editor/admin/owner allowed; non-member direct ids return leak-safe 404.

patch.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner may rename chats in the owning project; viewer receives 403 and cross-project chat ids return leak-safe 404.

delete.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner mutation on the owning project resource; viewer receives 403 and non-member direct ids return 404.

get.summary: List messages in a chat session.
get.x-authz: Project member read on the owning resource: viewer/editor/admin/owner allowed; non-member direct ids return leak-safe 404.

get.description: Reads a persisted chat artifact created by an assistant turn. Clients discover artifact ids from ChatMessage.references, ChatTurnSnapshot.artifacts or artifact.ready SSE events, not by parsing assistant text.
get.x-authz: Project member read on the owning resource: viewer/editor/admin/owner allowed; non-member direct ids return leak-safe 404.

get.summary: List draft agent suggestions for a chapter.

parameters.name: batchId
parameters.description: Filter to suggestions created in the specified backend suggestion batch.

get.x-authz: Project member read on the owning resource: viewer/editor/admin/owner allowed; non-member direct ids return leak-safe 404.
get.description: Lists persisted backend-created suggestions for a chapter. Clients may filter by the assistant turn or batch identifiers carried in structured message references and turn snapshots.

get.x-authz: Project member read on the owning resource: viewer/editor/admin/owner allowed; non-member direct ids return leak-safe 404.

post.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner mutation on the owning project resource; viewer receives 403 and non-member direct ids return 404.
Editor/admin/owner may reject a pending suggestion with expectedChapterRevision; stale revisions or terminal suggestions return 409 and cross-project ids return 404.
post.description: Rejects a pending backend-created suggestion with the same expectedChapterRevision guard used by approval. Stale revisions or terminal suggestions return 409 Problem.

get.description: Returns sanitized user/session metadata only. No cookies, JWTs, refresh tokens, invite secrets or raw CSRF secrets are present. Cache-Control: no-store.
get.x-authz: Authenticated session owner reads only their own user/session envelope; no project membership or direct-ID lookup is involved.

get.description: Returns opaque CSRF material with no-store caching and no session secret exposure. Token rotates around login/logout/session rotation.
get.x-authz: Anonymous CSRF bootstrap returns only opaque no-store token material; it grants no project or session authority.

post.description: Rotates the server session and refreshes CSRF material after sensitive auth state changes.
post.x-authz: Authenticated session owner only; anonymous callers receive 401 and no project authorization is evaluated.

get.description: Owner/admin project member listing; viewer/editor callers are denied to avoid unnecessary participant PII exposure.
get.x-authz: Owner/admin manage or list members; editor/viewer denied for mutations; cross-project member ids return leak-safe 404.

patch.description: Owner/admin role management. Owner membership is immutable; ownership transfer/demotion is out of scope and returns 409.
patch.x-authz: Owner/admin member role update; owner membership is immutable and ownership transfer is out of scope; cross-project member ids return leak-safe 404.

delete.description: Immediately removes non-owner access; owner/last-owner removal is out of scope and returns 409. Audit retains redacted access-control event.
delete.x-authz: Owner/admin member removal; owner/last-owner removal is rejected with 409 and cross-project member ids return leak-safe 404.

get.description: Owner/admin only. Does not expose raw invite tokens; invitation email PII is hidden from viewer/editor roles.
get.x-authz: Owner/admin manage invitations; invited authenticated verified-email user may accept; viewer/editor denied with 403 and cross-project ids return 404.

post.description: Owner/admin only. Invite token is unguessable, stored hashed and never returned.
post.x-authz: Owner/admin manage invitations; invited authenticated verified-email user may accept; viewer/editor denied with 403 and cross-project ids return 404.

post.description: Owner/admin only; canceled/expired outcomes use leak-safe stable problem codes.
post.x-authz: Owner/admin manage invitations; invited authenticated verified-email user may accept; viewer/editor denied with 403 and cross-project ids return 404.

post.description: Authenticated user accepts an opaque invitation id discovered through listMyProjectInvitations; provider-verified canonical email must match the invitation email, otherwise responses are leak-safe 404/403.
post.x-authz: Invited authenticated user accepts only an opaque invitation id discovered through verified-email invite discovery; owner/admin management ids do not bypass email match and cross-email ids return leak-safe 404.

get.description: Shared lifecycle for import, indexing, export, chat turn and agent run work.
get.x-authz: Project members may read visible non-sensitive job metadata; export result links remain owner/admin only.

post.description: Idempotent start command returning the shared Job envelope.
post.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner may start indexing; viewer is read-only and receives 403; cross-project project ids return 404.

get.description: Returns current job state. Direct-ID access resolves owning project membership and uses leak-safe 404 for inaccessible jobs. Export job downloadUrl/results are owner/admin only; editor/viewer reads omit restricted result data or return 403/404.
get.x-authz: Project members may read visible job metadata; export download links are owner/admin only and cross-tenant job ids return 404.

post.description: Idempotent cancel. Terminal jobs remain immutable and return current state.
post.x-authz: Job creator or project admin/owner may cancel; unauthorized project members receive 403 and cross-tenant job ids return 404.

post.description: Command creates persisted user message and async turn job. It does not stream response bytes; clients follow links.events or links.poll.
post.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner may create assistant work; viewer receives 403 and cross-project ids return 404.

get.description: Polling recovery for turn state, latest event id and persisted message/artifact/suggestion resources.
get.x-authz: Project members may read authorized chat turns; cross-tenant turn ids return leak-safe 404.

get.description: Resumable SSE. Last-Event-ID and afterEventId resume from durable event log; event log expiry returns 410 stream_expired and clients recover with GET /chat-turns/{turnId}.

schema.description: Server-sent event frames; each data payload conforms to ChatTurnEventEnvelope.

get.x-authz: Project members may read authorized chat turns; cross-tenant turn ids return leak-safe 404.

post.description: Starts persisted suggestion generation as a shared Job. Suggestions are durable resources; raw prompts and runtime traces are not exposed in canonical payloads.
post.x-authz: Editor/admin/owner may create assistant work; viewer receives 403 and cross-project ids return 404.

get.summary: List invitations addressed to current user.
get.description: Authenticated user with provider-verified canonical email lists opaque invitation ids addressed to that email, including leak-safe project and inviter context needed before accepting. Acceptance credential material is never returned or accepted in URLs.

200.description: Invitations addressed to current user email.

get.x-authz: Authenticated user must have provider-verified canonical email; only invitations addressed to that email are returned and raw accept tokens are never exposed.

sessionAuth.name: ck_session
sessionAuth.description: HttpOnly, Secure outside local development, SameSite=Lax or stricter, host-only cookie scoped to the application path. Default Max-Age is 12h; rememberMe may extend to 30d. Login/register rotate the session and refresh CSRF material; logout invalidates the server session.

Cursor.name: cursor
Cursor.description: Opaque cursor returned as PageMeta.nextCursor; invalid or mismatched cursors return 400 Problem.

CsrfToken.name: X-CSRF-Token
CsrfToken.description: Opaque CSRF token from GET /auth/csrf. Examples never contain real token values.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Scoped by principal + method + path + request fingerprint. Duplicate same-payload replay returns the original/current result; same-key mismatched payload returns 409 Problem.

LastEventId.name: Last-Event-ID
LastEventId.description: Native SSE resume cursor. Event ids exclude LF, CR and NUL; sequence is render order, not the resume cursor.

AfterEventId.name: afterEventId
AfterEventId.description: Fallback resume cursor for clients that cannot set Last-Event-ID.

InvitationId.name: invitationId
InvitationId.description: Opaque project invitation identifier. Invitees discover it through verified-email invitation listing; acceptance credential material is never returned or accepted in URLs.

Gone.description: Resource or stream event log expired

value.title: Resource or stream event log expired

Id.description: Opaque stable identifier. Implementations may use prefixed IDs such as `book_...`, `chapter_...`, or `paragraph_...`; clients must not parse semantics from the ID string.

Problem.description: RFC 9457-style problem details with stable machine code and redacted request id.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor bound to request fingerprint.

email.description: Provider-verified canonical email used for invitation discovery and acceptance. Backends expose this after identity-provider verification; the API does not define a custom email-verification flow.

emailVerified.description: True only when the identity provider has verified the canonical email currently exposed on the session user.

rememberMe.description: Caller preference for a longer-lived session. The server may use it to choose session duration without exposing token mechanics.

currentMembership.description: Membership and permission context of the authenticated caller in this project, returned without exposing the full member list to viewer/editor callers.

IndexingSummary.description: Stable backend index state for the book. Active import/indexing progress, cancellation and errors are exposed through the shared Job lifecycle.

order.description: One-based book order inside the project.

displayNumber.description: Stable volume ordinal such as `I`, `II`, `III`, or `IV`; clients may derive local presentation labels.

afterBookId.description: Insert/reorder the new volume after this book; null inserts at the beginning.
Move this volume after another book; null moves it to the beginning.

maxFileSizeBytes.description: Maximum accepted upload size in bytes.

displayNumber.description: Stable chapter ordinal used for ordering and references.
Visible chapter number/label used by clients for chapter navigation.

position.description: One-based chapter position in the current book.

total.description: Total chapters in the current book.

previous.description: Previous chapter summary; null at the beginning of a book.

next.description: Next chapter summary; null at the end of a book.

revision.description: Revision of the payload returned for the selected content variant.

text.description: Plain text derived from markdown for search, references, and compact previews.

markdown.description: Canonical editable paragraph content in the supported manuscript markdown profile: bold, italic, heading, quote, list item, scene break, and link markup.

ChapterParagraphInput.description: Input shape for draft saves. Existing paragraphs keep `id`; newly inserted paragraphs omit `id` until the backend assigns stable paragraph IDs after save/indexing.

text.description: Optional plain-text mirror; backend may derive it from markdown.

markdown.description: Canonical editable paragraph content in the supported manuscript markdown profile.

ChapterSummary.description: Lightweight chapter structure item for backend navigation and chapter lists. Full paragraph content is returned only by `/chapters/{chapterId}`.

displayNumber.description: Stable chapter ordinal used for ordering and references.

expectedDraftRevision.description: Draft revision expected by the caller for publication. The server returns 409 if the draft changed.

TextRange.description: Optional precision range inside the paragraph identified by a surrounding ReaderLocator.

quote.description: Bounded selected text preview; not a full manuscript body.

targetView.description: Text version this locator points to. Public references normally use `published`; draft selections use `draft`.

paragraphId.description: Stable paragraph anchor used by assistant references when the reference points to a specific paragraph.

ReaderLocator.description: Canonical reader locator. Source context belongs in wrapper resources such as annotations, search results or chat artifacts, not in this canonical anchor.

targetView.description: Text version this locator points to. Public references normally use `published`; draft selections use `draft`.

paragraphId.description: Required stable paragraph anchor for interactive reader references from chat/search artifacts.

quote.description: Bounded selected text preview shown on cards; null for pure bookmarks.

body.description: User note text; null for highlights/bookmarks without note text.
Optional note text attached to the highlight.

quote.description: Selected text quoted by the note when created from a reader selection.
Bounded client-selected quote used only for locator validation.
Bounded client-selected quote used only for locator validation.
Bounded client-selected quote used only for locator validation.
Bounded client-selected quote used only for locator validation.
Bounded client-selected quote used only for locator validation.
Bounded client-selected quote used only for locator validation.

excerpt.description: Bounded search snippet; not a full manuscript body.

cursorFingerprint.description: Server fingerprint binding cursor to request body.

snippetPolicy.description: Snippets are bounded synthetic/redacted excerpts, not full manuscript bodies.

tasks.description: Requested backend analysis tasks, e.g. canon, punctuation, or style.

text.description: Canonical persisted part text; clients derive local labels from type/status/text.

sequence.description: Stable ordering copied from stream events where available.

content.description: Assistant/user text content. Artifact and suggestion references are persisted as structured resources and event payloads, not embedded text directives.

parts.description: Persisted visible text parts after the turn completes.

references.description: Backend-owned references to artifacts or suggestions associated with the message.

ChatArtifactKind.description: Structured chat artifact category; clients discover it from persisted resources or events, not assistant text markers.

quote.description: Bounded reader-reference quote preview; not a full manuscript body.

targetView.description: Text version this locator points to. Public references normally use `published`; draft selections use `draft`.

AgentDiffLocator.description: Draft locator precise enough to render and apply an agent diff hunk.

before.description: Bounded before text for one proposed hunk; not a full manuscript body.

after.description: Bounded after text for one proposed hunk; not a full manuscript body.

baseChapterRevision.description: Chapter revision used by the backend/agent when it created this suggestion.

batchId.description: Suggestion batch created by one assistant turn or backend agent run.

sourceMessageId.description: Assistant message associated with the backend-created suggestion, when available.

contextQuote.description: Bounded visible context for the suggestion card; raw prompts and runtime traces are not exposed.

expectedChapterRevision.description: Current chapter revision known by the client before applying the suggestion.

chapter.description: Updated chapter after accept; null after reject.

csrfToken.description: Opaque token example for docs; never a session secret.

title.description: Leak-safe project title shown to the invited user before accepting.

id.description: Opaque invitation id; raw accept token is never returned.

cancel.description: Canonical API cancel URL when the job can be canceled.

result.description: Short-lived owner/admin-only canonical API result URL; null unless a permitted result link is ready.

ChatTurnEventEnvelope.description: Discriminated chat turn SSE payload. Raw prompts, internal runtime traces and manuscript excerpts are not streamed as diagnostics.

ChatMessageReferenceKind.description: Structured backend-owned message reference category. These values are not assistant text markers.

kind.description: Message reference points to a persisted reader-reference artifact.

artifactId.description: Persisted chat artifact id; fetch with `/chat-artifacts/{artifactId}`.

kind.description: Message reference points to backend-created agent suggestions.

chapterId.description: Chapter containing the backend-created suggestions; list with `/chapters/{chapterId}/agent-suggestions`.

suggestionBatchId.description: Backend-created suggestion batch associated with one assistant turn.

suggestionIds.description: Specific backend-created suggestions associated with the assistant turn.

downloadUrl.description: Short-lived owner/admin-only canonical API export download link; null for non-ready or redacted responses.

JobResult.description: Bounded job metadata only. Raw manuscript content, runtime traces and unrestricted export payloads are not canonical job result data.

expectedChapterRevision.description: Current chapter revision known by the client before rejecting the suggestion.
